report_name: NightSpire Ransomware Claims Attack on French Org, Threatens to Leak Audit Data

threat_actors.name: NightSpire
threat_actors.description: A ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group that claimed responsibility for the cyberattack against Association OCACIA. The group uses double-extortion tactics, operating a data leak site to pressure victims into paying ransoms.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of victim organizations through data encryption and the threat of leaking exfiltrated sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The NightSpire group exfiltrated sensitive internal documents, including audit reports and non-compliance records, from Association OCACIA's network before deploying the encryption payload.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The attackers deployed NightSpire ransomware to encrypt files on Association OCACIA's network, causing business disruption and serving as a primary component of their extortion tactic.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware payload likely deleted volume shadow copies using vssadmin.exe to prevent easy restoration of encrypted files from local backups.

malware.name: NightSpire Ransomware
malware.description: A ransomware payload deployed by the NightSpire group to encrypt victim files. It renames files with the '.nightspire' extension and is used in double-extortion attacks involving data exfiltration.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by the ransomware to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery from local backups.

identities.name: Association OCACIA
identities.description: A French organization involved in auditing and certification that was targeted by the NightSpire ransomware group. The attackers claimed to have exfiltrated sensitive audit and compliance data.

infrastructure.name: NightSpire Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A data leak site operated by the NightSpire ransomware group to publish threats and exfiltrated data from victims who do not pay the ransom, such as Association OCACIA.

indicators.name: NightSpire Ransomware File Extension
indicators.pattern:
title: NightSpire Ransomware File Extension
id: 2b9c1d4e-8f6a-4b1c-9d3e-5a7b6c8d9e0f
status: experimental
description: Detects files being renamed with the .nightspire extension, which is a strong indicator of the NightSpire ransomware encryption process.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/nightspire-ransomware-targets-association-ocacia-ocacia-org/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.nightspire'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation or renaming of files to include the '.nightspire' extension, indicating active encryption by NightSpire ransomware.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-f6a7-b8c9-d0e1f2a3b4c5
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/nightspire-ransomware-targets-association-ocacia-ocacia-org/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'vssadmin.exe' with commands to delete volume shadow copies, a common tactic used by ransomware like NightSpire to hamper recovery efforts.
